chief_complaint: A 30-year-old woman presented with nausea, vomiting, and palpitations progressing to cardiogenic shock.
history: The patient had a history of non-specific symptoms, including nausea, vomiting, and palpitations. She was previously healthy and had no known medical conditions. She was not on any medications. Her social history was unremarkable.
exam: Vital signs: blood pressure 80/40 mmHg, heart rate 120 bpm, respiratory rate 24 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed severely impaired biventricular function.
labs: ECG: showing signs of cardiogenic shock. Blood tests: elevated troponin levels, normal electrolytes. Imaging: echocardiogram showing severely impaired biventricular function. Other tests: catecholamine levels were elevated.
differentials: Cardiogenic shock due to myocardial infarction
correct_ranking: Cardiogenic shock due to myocardial infarction
